content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of integers. Your task is to sort the array such that all odd numbers come before all even numbers while maintaining the original relative order of the odd and even numbers.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer NNN (the array size).
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the elements of the array.


Output Format
Output the sorted array where all odd numbers appear before all even numbers, maintaining the relative order.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array, and the second line contains N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the array after reordering: all odd numbers appear before all even numbers while preserving the original relative order inside each group. Numbers are separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Odds appear in original order 3,1,5 followed by evens 2,4.
All odds (1,3,5) keep their order, then all evens (2,4,6).

solution.approach: Traverse the array once and collect odd numbers in a list preserving their order, then collect even numbers in another list. Finally output the concatenation of the odd list followed by the even list. This stable partition works in O(N) time per test case and uses O(N) extra space for the two lists.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for storing odds and evens.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException{BufferedReader br = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder out = new StringBuilder();int T = Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- > 0){int N = Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringTokenizer st = new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());List<String> odds = new ArrayList<>();List<String> evens = new ArrayList<>();for(int i = 0; i < N; i++){int x = Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());if((x & 1) == 1) odds.add(String.valueOf(x));else evens.add(String.valueOf(x));}boolean first = true;for(String s : odds){if(!first) out.append(' ');out.append(s);first = false;}for(String s : evens){if(!first) out.append(' ');out.append(s);first = false;}out.append('\n');}System.out.print(out.toString());}}
